title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 8)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's 854 Brockton Ave. sold for $550,000; built in 1900 with 2 bedrooms on a 10,871-square-foot lot.
Allston's 147 Kelton St. #609 condo sold for $635,000; built in 1988 with 2 bedrooms on a 1,014-square-foot lot.
In Boston, a condo at 1 Dalton St. sold for $14,000,000, indicating a high-end real estate transaction.

title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville's Newbury Street location frequently has lines due to its unique 'one size fits all' clothing policy.
Blank Street coffee on Boylston Street employs line security due to the popularity and crowds at its Copley location.
Brick Street Bagels and North End's Italian confection shops like Modern Pastry attract long lines despite differing opinions on value.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for cosmetic injections at her two Massachusetts spas.
Fadanelli imported counterfeit Botox and fillers from China and Brazil, earning over $1 million despite lacking a medical license.
Fadanelli faces up to 20 years in prison and a $250,000 fine for her illegal importation activities.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jacob Graves, 29, was fatally shot by Boston police after wielding a 'sword-like weapon' during a mental health crisis.
The incident occurred near Northeastern University; multiple officers and emergency responders were injured but not seriously.
Boston police and the Suffolk County DA’s office are investigating the shooting, with officer identities not yet released.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens dismisses rumors about leaving Celtics for college basketball roles like UNC's coaching position.
Stevens emphasizes commitment to the Celtics, where he transitioned from head coach to president of basketball operations in 2021.
Rumors of Stevens' departure arise periodically due to his success but he remains focused on NBA duties.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, sentenced to life with parole possibility after 15 years for killing Maceo Martin in 2024.
Martin was fatally shot outside his ex-wife's Cambridge apartment while yelling insults and ringing doorbell.
Lapaix convicted of second-degree murder following a nine-day trial, prosecution cites alternative handling of dispute.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy, accused of killing her three children, will admit guilt if her trial is split into two parts.
Clancy's defense strategy relies on an insanity plea and argues her state of mind is the key issue.
Prosecutors oppose splitting the trial, insisting on presenting evidence of premeditation and criminal responsibility together.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots traded linebacker Marte Mapu to the Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round pick.
Mapu, a 2023 third-round pick, played 17 games primarily on special teams with New England.
The trade frees a roster spot and saves the Patriots $1.5 million in cap space.

title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: CCRI's Warwick campus was locked down following reports of a suspicious man with a gun in nearby woods.
Police used K-9 units and drones to search for the suspect, discovering a backpack with an airsoft gun and pellet rifle.
Classes were canceled for the day, and police continue to search for the suspect with information on his identity.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas experienced a setback in his recovery from a rib muscle strain while rehabbing a knee injury.
Casas, once the Red Sox's promising first baseman, has struggled with injuries, appearing in only 92 games since 2024.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora stated Casas' recovery didn't progress as expected and he will undergo imaging this week.
